message:
chore: add .editorconfig + .gitattributes for cross-editor / cross-OS hygiene (br-frankenpandas-xgsf)

Closes the non-Rust style-drift gap. Before this commit, YAML /
Markdown / Python / JSON files in the workspace drifted silently
across editors — indent widths, trailing whitespace, newline-at-EOF.
The active multi-agent swarm commits across all these file types.

.editorconfig:
- charset=utf-8, end_of_line=lf, insert_final_newline=true,
  trim_trailing_whitespace=true on all files.
- 4-space default indent; 2-space override for .yml/.yaml/.md/
  .json/.toml (industry convention for those formats).
- tab indent for Makefiles (required by make).
- insert_final_newline=false for .jsonl (bead store's append-only
  invariant requires no trailing newline).

.gitattributes:
- `* text=auto eol=lf` normalizes line endings to LF on commit —
  prerequisite for br-0l5r (cross-platform CI matrix) landing
  Windows runners without CRLF silently creeping in.
- Explicit source-file classifications (.rs, .py, .toml, .yml,
  .yaml, .sh, .md, .json, .jsonl as text eol=lf).
- Explicit binary classifications for fixture formats we track
  (.png, .webp, .xlsx, .xls, .parquet, .feather, .arrow, etc.)
  so git doesn't attempt line-ending normalization on them.
- Belt-and-suspenders: scripts/* and *.sh re-declared as text
  eol=lf so Windows contributors don't silently break shebangs.

Zero source code changes. Zero test changes. Pure hygiene.

Related:
- br-0l5r (cross-platform CI) — prerequisite alignment; Windows
  contributors will pick up these rules automatically.
- br-thty (rustfmt.toml) — companion style-enforcement for Rust;
  rustfmt.toml is a follow-up bead.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — will reference these files once
  that umbrella lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+root = true
+
+[*]
+charset = utf-8
+end_of_line = lf
+indent_style = space
+indent_size = 4
+insert_final_newline = true
+trim_trailing_whitespace = true
+
+[*.{yml,yaml,md,json,toml}]
+indent_size = 2
+
+[*.rs]
+indent_size = 4
+
+[*.py]
+indent_size = 4
+
+[Makefile]
+indent_style = tab
+
+[*.jsonl]
+insert_final_newline = false

@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Normalize line endings across OS checkouts (matters once br-0l5r's
+# cross-platform CI matrix lands with Windows runners).
+* text=auto eol=lf
+
+# Source files — always LF.
+*.rs    text eol=lf
+*.py    text eol=lf
+*.toml  text eol=lf
+*.yml   text eol=lf
+*.yaml  text eol=lf
+*.sh    text eol=lf
+*.md    text eol=lf
+*.json  text eol=lf
+*.jsonl text eol=lf
+
+# Binary files — never touched by git's EOL normalization.
+*.png     binary
+*.webp    binary
+*.jpg     binary
+*.jpeg    binary
+*.gif     binary
+*.xlsx    binary
+*.xls     binary
+*.xlsb    binary
+*.ods     binary
+*.parquet binary
+*.feather binary
+*.arrow   binary
+*.sqlite  binary
+*.db      binary
+
+# Shell scripts keep UNIX line endings even on Windows checkouts.
+*.sh text eol=lf
+scripts/* text eol=lf
